channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Apache fixes critical HTTP/2 double-free flaw CVE-2026-23918 enabling RCE
item.description: Apache fixed several flaws in HTTP Server, including CVE-2026-23918 (CVSS score of 8.8), a double-free bug in HTTP/2 that could allow remote code execution. The Apache Software Foundation has released updates to fix m...

item.title: Intel 471 speeds threat hunting and remediation with Retroactive Threat Detections
item.description: Intel 471 has announced Retroactive Threat Detections (RTD), a new capability within its Verity471 platform. RTD helps security teams quickly understand the impact of new threats on their environments. This transforms...

item.title: Rowhammer Attack Against NVIDIA Chips
item.description: A new rowhammer attack gives complete control of NVIDIA CPUs. On Thursday, two research teams, working independently of each other, demonstrated attacks against two cards from Nvidia’s Ampere generation that take GPU...

item.title: Extreme Networks introduces Agent ONE for autonomous enterprise networking
item.description: Extreme Networks has introduced Extreme Agent ONE, a new class of AI agents for enterprise networking. Moving beyond generic, prompt-based AI, Extreme Agent ONE runs on the Extreme AI stack purpose-built for enterpris...

item.title: UiPath adds agentic AI capabilities to Automation Suite for government agencies
item.description: UiPath has announced the release of agentic AI capabilities on UiPath Automation Suite. The Automation Suite updates help government agencies and regulated industries accelerate agentic AI and automation adoption and...

item.title: CISA: Critical Infrastructure Must Master Isolation, Recovery
item.description: The agency has issued guidance to help critical infrastructure operators prepare for cyberattacks by foreign threat actors. The post CISA: Critical Infrastructure Must Master Isolation, Recovery appeared first on Secu...

item.title: 8×8 updates CX platform with AI, analytics, and frontline management capabilities
item.description: 8×8 has released a set of platform updates to the 8×8 Platform for CX that target the operational gaps most commonly stalling organizations, including AI deployments requiring months of integration, queues IT teams ca...

item.title: Proton Mail brings quantum-safe email encryption to all accounts
item.description: Post-quantum protection is now available as an optional feature in Proton Mail across all plans, including the free tier. How post-quantum protection works Once enabled, Proton Mail generates new encryption keys desig...

item.title: Insights into the clustering and reuse of phone numbers in scam emails
item.description: Talos has recently started to collect and gather intelligence around phone numbers within emails as an additional indicator of compromise (IOC). In this blog, we discuss new insights into in-the-wild phone number reus...

item.title: Root-level RCE vulnerability in Palo Alto firewalls exploited (CVE-2026-0300)
item.description: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-0300) affecting Palo Alto Networks firewalls is being actively exploited by attackers, the security company acknowledged today, and urged customers to implement mitigations as they a...

item.title: New Relic advances AI observability with new intelligence layer
item.description: New Relic has announced New Relic Knowledge, a new platform capability that integrates telemetry and knowledge sources to enhance issue detection and resolution. By combining real-time telemetry with historical incide...

item.title: Sophisticated Quasar Linux RAT Targets Software Developers
item.description: The persistent, evasive implant provides remote access, surveillance, and credential exfiltration capabilities. The post Sophisticated Quasar Linux RAT Targets Software Developers appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: groundcover expands its observability platform with enhanced Synthetic Monitoring and RUM
item.description: groundcover has expanded its capabilities with new and enhanced offerings across Synthetic Monitoring and Real User Monitoring (RUM). These innovations give engineering teams greater visibility into the user experienc...

item.title: ServiceNow strengthens enterprise AI security with Autonomous Security & Risk platform
item.description: ServiceNow has launched Autonomous Security & Risk to govern every AI agent, identity, and connected asset. Armis delivers continuous asset intelligence across code, IT, OT, IoT, and connected assets, while Veza provi...

item.title: Google's Android Apps Get Public Verification to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Google has announced expanded Binary Transparency for Android as a way to safeguard the ecosystem from supply chain attacks. "This new public ledger ensures the Google apps on your device are exactly what we intended...

item.title: Megaport enhances network resilience with integrated DDoS protection
item.description: Megaport has announced the launch of Megaport DDoS Protection. This new built-in security capability for Megaport Internet allows customers to filter malicious traffic directly within the Megaport network, rather than...

item.title: One in Eight Workers Has Sold Their Corporate Logins
item.description: Cifas says that 13% of employees admit selling company credentials to a former colleague

item.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an intrusion that involved the use of a CloudZ remote access tool (RAT) and a previous undocumented plugin dubbed Pheno with the aim of facilitating credential theft...

item.title: Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack
item.description: While trojanized Daemon Tools versions were installed worldwide, a sophisticated backdoor was dropped only on a dozen systems. The post Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack appeared...

item.title: India orders infosec red alert in case Mythos sparks crime spree
item.description: Securities regulator urges market players to develop new strategies and nail cyber-basics before AI models fuel mass attacks

item.title: Malicious PyTorch Lightning update hits AI supply chain security
item.description: A malicious PyTorch Lightning update (v2.6.3) on PyPI spread briefly, stealing credentials and raising major concerns about AI supply chain security. A malicious update of the PyTorch Lightning library exposed develop...

item.title: Oracle Debuts Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates
item.description: Containing fixes for critical-severity vulnerabilities, the monthly rollouts will focus on addressing priority issues faster. The post Oracle Debuts Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates appeared first on SecurityWe...

item.title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
item.description: As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure.

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 6th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9920, (Wed, May 6th)
Instructure hacker claims data theft from 8,800 schools, universities
item.description: The hacker behind a breach at education technology giant Instructure claims to have stolen 280 million data records for students and staff from 8,809 colleges, school districts, and online education platforms. [...]

item.title: U.S. court sentences Karakurt ransomware negotiator to 8.5 years
item.description: Deniss Zolotarjovs was sentenced to 8.5 years in the U.S. after pleading guilty to money laundering and fraud tied to ransomware. Deniss Zolotarjovs, a Latvian national linked to the Karakurt ransomware gang, has been...

item.title: ServiceNow clears agents for landing with new AI control tower
item.description: ServiceNow acquisitions Veza and Traceloop join to monitor agents and AI workflows

item.title: Microsoft Flags Mass Phishing Campaign Using Fake Compliance Emails
item.description: Microsoft researchers warn of a large-scale phishing campaign using fake compliance emails to steal credentials, targeting 35,000 users across 13,000 organizations worldwide

item.title: Attackers are cashing in on fresh 'CopyFail' Linux flaw
item.description: Researchers dropped a reliable root exploit and it didn’t sit idle for long

item.title: Real estate giant confirms vishing incident as ShinyHunters and Qilin both come knocking
item.description: Cushman & Wakefield activated incident response protocols after serial extortionists issued separate threats

item.title: Vimeo data breach exposes personal information of 119,000 people
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information belonging to over 119,000 people after hacking the Vimeo online video platform in April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

item.title: Critical Remote Code Execution Vulnerability Patched in Android
item.description: CVE-2026-0073 affects Android’s System component and it can be exploited without any user interaction. The post Critical Remote Code Execution Vulnerability Patched in Android appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
item.description: Every AI tool, workflow automation, and productivity app your employees connected to Google or Microsoft this year left something behind: a persistent OAuth token with no expiration date, no automatic cleanup, and in...

item.title: Romance scammers turn sweet talk into £102M payday
item.description: Victims losing £280K a day to fake profiles and sob stories

item.title: ScarCruft hackers push BirdCall Android malware via game platform
item.description: The North Korean hacker group APT37 has been delivering an Android version of a backdoor called BirdCall in a supply-chain attack through a video game platform. [...]

item.title: OWASP Foundation Unveils Its Strategic Plan for a World Without Insecure Software
item.description: The OWASP Foundation is entering a defining moment. This strategic plan outlines how OWASP will move from being a recognized voice in security to a truly transformative force in the industry. Inside this document, you...

item.title: Singapore boffins get diverse SIEMs singing in harmony with agentic rule translation
item.description: Vendors all use different formats. This tech translates them all so you can smooth your SOC

item.title: MetInfo, Weaver E-cology Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs
item.description: The security defects allow unauthenticated, remote attackers to execute arbitrary code through crafted requests. The post MetInfo, Weaver E-cology Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Trellix Reveals Unauthorized Access to Source Code
item.description: Security vendor Trellix has suffered a breach involving unauthorized access

item.title: Copy Fail: What You Need to Know About the Most Severe Linux Threat in Years
item.description: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431) is a critical Linux kernel LPE that allows stealthy root access. This flaw impacts millions of systems. Read our analysis. The post Copy Fail: What You Need to Know About the Most Severe Lin...

item.title: Introducing AI traffic analysis dashboards for AWS WAF
item.description: As AI agents, bots, and programmatic access become an increasingly significant portion of web traffic, organizations need better tools to understand, analyze, and manage this activity. Today, we’re excited to announce...

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of April 27, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info n/a-- OVMS3 3.3.005 Buffer overflow vulnerability in Open Vehicle Monitoring System 3 (OVMS3) 3.3.005. In canforma...

item.title: The EOL Blind Spot in Your CVE Feed: What SCA Tools Miss
item.description: Critical vulnerabilities can exist in open source software your scanners don't check. HeroDevs reveals how EOL software creates blind spots in CVE feeds and SCA tools, and how you can receive a free end-of-life scan f...

item.title: Google now offers up to $1.5 million for some Android exploits
item.description: Google overhauls its Android and Chrome vulnerability rewards programs, offering bounties of up to $1.5 million for the most difficult exploits while scaling back payouts for flaws that artificial intelligence (AI) ha...

item.title: Tanium Atlas aims to accelerate threat response in the AI era
item.description: Tanium announced Tanium Atlas, an autonomous operating system (OS) that gives a single IT or security operator the data, guidance and reach to accomplish what once required an entire team – moving from intent to outco...

item.title: Unpatched flaws turn Ollama’s auto-updater into a persistent RCE vector, researchers say
item.description: Researchers at Striga have disclosed two vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-42248, CVE-2026-42249) in Ollama’s Windows auto-updater that, when chained together, may allow an attacker to covertly plant a persistent executable t...

item.title: LastPass Mobile Smart Scanner improves password security
item.description: LastPass has launched Mobile Smart Scanner, a solution that converts photographs of typed or handwritten credentials into structured, ready-to-use password entries that can be reviewed, saved, and autofilled directly...

item.title: Five ways to use Kiro and Amazon Q to strengthen your security posture
item.description: A Monday morning security alert flags unauthorized access attempts, security group misconfigurations, and AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy violations. Your team needs answers fast. Security teams are us...

item.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
item.description: A proof-of-concept exploit (PoC) shows how someone with admin privileges can exploit the issue to steal passwords, and thus use them to engage in further malicious activity.

item.title: Hacker Conversations: Joey Melo on Hacking AI
item.description: AI red team specialist details his methods for manipulating AI guardrails through jailbreaking and data poisoning, helping developers harden machine learning models. The post Hacker Conversations: Joey Melo on Hacking...

item.title: Introducing Penetration Test Findings: Unified Offensive Security in Wiz
item.description: Streamline pen-testing by unifying findings from bug bounties, manual audits, and Wiz Red Agent into a single, context-rich view.

item.description: Center for Internet Security helps organizations deploy AI and high-performance compute environments from a trusted, hardened operating system baseline. CIS Hardened Images help teams reduce misconfiguration risk, sup...

item.title: Critical Bug Could Expose 300,000 Ollama Deployments to Information Theft
item.description: Dubbed Bleeding Llama, the heap out-of-bounds read issue can be exploited remotely, without authentication. The post Critical Bug Could Expose 300,000 Ollama Deployments to Information Theft appeared first on Security...

item.title: A Walkthrough of the 2026 Global Cybersecurity Summit Agenda
item.description: The full agenda for the Rapid7 2026 Global Cybersecurity Summit is now live, and it gives a clearer sense of how the conversation around security operations is evolving. Across two days, the sessions progress from a s...

item.title: VIAVI CyberFlood CF1000 pushes 400G validation for multi-terabit AI data centers
item.description: VIAVI Solutions has announced the launch of its next-generation CyberFlood CF1000 Appliance, a native 400G security and application performance test platform for the validation of multi-terabit security and AI data ce...

item.title: AI Adoption Outpaces Safety Policies, Leaving Organizations Exposed to Cyber Risk
item.description: ISACA report warns that while AI has become the norm, many organizations are yet to formally apply safety or security policies around its use

item.title: We Scanned 1 Million Exposed AI Services. Here's How Bad the Security Actually Is
item.description: While the software industry has made genuine strides over the past few decades to deliver products securely, the furious pace of AI adoption is putting that progress at risk. Businesses are moving fast to self-host LL...

item.title: DarkSword Malware
item.description: DarkSword is a sophisticated piece of malware —probably government designed—that targets iOS. Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified a new iOS full-chain exploit that leveraged multiple zero-day vulner...

item.title: Anomali ThreatStream Next-Gen speeds threat response across workflows
item.description: Anomali has announced ThreatStream Next-Gen. Available standalone or within the Anomali Unified Security Data Lake, it turns threat intelligence into an active decisioning layer across security workflows, validated to...

item.title: CloudZ RAT potentially steals OTP messages using Pheno plugin
item.description: Cisco Talos discovered an intrusion, active since at least January 2026, where an unknown attacker implanted a CloudZ remote access tool (RAT) and a previously undocumented plugin called “Pheno.”

item.title: NCSC Warns of an AI-Fuelled “Vulnerability Patch Wave”
item.description: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre is urging organizations to prepare for glut of new software updates

item.title: WhatsApp Discloses File Spoofing, Arbitrary URL Scheme Vulnerabilities
item.description: The vulnerabilities were reported to Meta through its bug bounty program and were patched with updates released earlier this year. The post WhatsApp Discloses File Spoofing, Arbitrary URL Scheme Vulnerabilities appear...

item.title: North Korean hackers trojanize gaming platform to spy on ethnic Koreans in China
item.description: A gaming platform built for ethnic Koreans in China has been serving backdoored Windows and Android software to its users since late 2024. The platform, sqgame[.]net, hosts traditional card and board games for a commu...

item.title: Meta adds proof-based security to encrypted backups
item.description: Meta has updated its infrastructure for protecting password-based and end-to-end encrypted backups, introducing over-the-air fleet key distribution for Messenger and a commitment to publishing evidence of secure fleet...

item.title: Can your coding style predict whether your code is vulnerable?
item.description: Developers leave fingerprints in the code they write. Naming choices, indentation patterns, preferred APIs, and the way someone structures a loop or handles a pointer all carry traces of individual habit. Researchers...

item.title: Security briefing: April 2026
item.description: Supply chain failure is the gift that keeps on giving, or should I say taking? The vulnerabilities, the exploitation, the breaches…these are inevitable, we know that. None of these are surprising anymore. The need for...

item.description: Elastic Security v9.4 introduces Entity Analytics Watchlists, a way to codify what your team already knows about high-risk entities and feed that context directly into risk scoring, without custom pipelines or detecti...

item.title: AI-generated hunting leads: The hunt starts before you ask the question
item.description: Introducing AI-generated hunting leads, proactive, environment-aware threat hypotheses powered by Elastic Entity analytics and integrated AI reasoning.

item.title: Building the business case for a secure open source supply chain
item.description: Learn how Kyndryl reframed open source security as a business driver — reducing risk, lowering costs, and accelerating developer productivity.

item.title: Shadow IT has given way to shadow AI. Enter AI-BOMs
item.description: 'If you don't have visibility, you can't understand what to protect'

item.title: Critical cPanel Vulnerability Weaponized to Target Government and MSP Networks
item.description: A previously unknown threat actor has been observed targeting government and military entities in Southeast Asia, alongside a smaller cluster of managed service providers (MSPs) and hosting providers in the Philippine...

item.title: If the vote you rocked, your personal info can be grokked
item.description: Even limited voter rolls can be linked to identify people, research shows

item.title: Five Eyes spook shops warn rapid rollouts of agentic AI are too risky
item.description: Prioritize resilience over productivity, say CISA, NCSC and their friends from Oz, NZ, Canada

item.title: Weaver E-cology critical bug exploited in attacks since March
item.description: Hackers have been exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-22679) in the Weaver E-cology office automation since mid-March to run discovery commands. [...]

item.title: Researchers report Amazon SES abused in phishing to evade detection
item.description: Cybersecurity firm Kaspersky reports that the Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) is being increasingly abused to send convincing phishing emails that can bypass standard security filters and render reputation-based blo...

item.title: MOVEit automation flaws could enable full system compromise
item.description: Progress fixes critical MOVEit Automation flaws, including an authentication bypass bug that could let attackers gain unauthorized access to systems. Progress Software addressed two vulnerabilities in MOVEit Automatio...

item.title: Physical Cargo Theft Gets a Boost From Cybercriminals
item.description: Cargo theft is no longer about small groups of criminals operating on the ground, but transnational cybercriminal syndicates using access to supply chain systems to reroute goods.

item.description: Penske Logistics has announced the launch of Supply Chain Insight, a secure technology platform and mobile application that provides customers with a real-time view of their supply chain operations across transportati...

item.title: DigiCert breached via malicious screensaver file
item.description: A targeted social engineering attack against DigiCert’s support channel led to the compromise of internal systems and the unauthorized issuance of EV Code Signing certificates. DigiCert is a global Certificate Authori...

item.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
item.description: Shortly after the authentication-bypass flaw was disclosed multiple proof-of-concept exploits appeared, and one researcher claims there's been zero-day activity for at least a month.

item.title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks
item.description: The acquisition strengthens Cisco’s push into identity-centric security for AI and machine access. The post Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Securing open proxies in your AWS environment
item.description: This article shows you how to identify and secure open proxies in your AWS environment to prevent abuse, protect your IP address reputation, and control costs. An open proxy is a server that forwards traffic on behalf...

item.title: What Is an Incident Response Plan?
item.description: Key Takeaways Security incidents do not wait for you to figure out who is responsible for what. The breach is happening, the clock is running, and the wrong call made at 2am under pressure can turn a containable event...

item.description: Key Takeaways Cloud data security is the discipline that closes this gap. It is not only a technical challenge but also a governance and human risk problem, where identity misuse and data movement are as critical as i...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
item.description: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches. While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open doors, and open-source...

item.title: Local Guardrails for Secrets Security in the Age of AI Coding Assistants
item.description: Modern developer environments expose sensitive context across files, prompts, logs, and commands. Learn how layered local controls reduce secrets risk.

item.title: They don’t hack, they borrow: How fraudsters target credit unions
item.description: Fraudsters aren't hacking credit unions, they are exploiting normal business processes. Flare reveals how structured loan fraud methods use stolen identities to pass verification and secure funds. [...]

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026
item.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Airbus, Cyera, Fortra, Palo Alto Networks, Silverfort, and Socket. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Webinar: Why MSPs must rethink security and backup strategies
item.description: Security breaches don't just test your defenses—they test your recovery. Join Kaseya in our upcoming webinar to learn how MSPs strengthen resilience with SaaS backups and BCDR to stay operational after attacks. [...]

item.title: From Foundation to Force: Your Guide to Operationalizing Wiz at Scale
item.description: Following your foundation, operationalize Wiz across development, detection and response, and program maturity so your security program never stops getting stronger.

item.description: Secure Microsoft 365 and the cloud it powers — one platform, one graph, complete context.

item.description: On December 4, 2025, a 17-year-old was arrested in Osaka under Japan’s Unauthorized Access Prohibition Act. The young man had run malicious code to extract the personal data of over 7 million users of Kaikatsu Club, J...

item.title: Microsoft confirms April Windows updates cause backup failures
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that the April 2026 security updates are causing failures in third-party backup applications using the psmounterex.sys driver. [...]

item.description: Polymarket is a platform where people can bet on real-world events, political and otherwise. Leaving the ethical considerations of this aside (for one, it facilitates assassination ), one of the issues with making thi...

item.description: Advanced Account Security provides stronger login methods, more secure account recovery, shorter sessions, and training exclusion. The post OpenAI Rolls Out Advanced Security for ChatGPT Accounts appeared first on Sec...

item.description: The attacks likely target CVE-2026-41940, a recently patched zero-day leading to administrative access. The post Over 40,000 Servers Compromised in Ongoing cPanel Exploitation appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Bluekit phishing kit enables automated phishing with 40+ templates and AI tools

item.title: Global Crackdown Arrests 276, Shuts 9 Crypto Scam Centers, Seizes $701M
item.description: A coordinated international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities has arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine scam centers used for cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes targeting Americans, res...

item.title: Pipelock: Open-source AI agent firewall
item.description: AI coding agents run with shell access, environment variables containing API keys, and unrestricted internet connectivity, creating a single point of failure where one compromised tool call can leak credentials to an...

item.description: Office work in 2026 runs through a stack of mobile apps that sit on the same phones people use for banking, messaging family, and tracking their location. Ten of the most common workplace apps in use across U.S. compa...

item.description: Conversational Entity Analytics delivers Entity Analytics features as rich inline attachments and Canvas previews into Agent Builder, so you don’t have to leave the conversation.

item.title: One agent, the right skills: Elastic Security 9.4 brings domain expertise on demand to every SOC workflow
item.description: Elastic Security 9.4 introduces skills, modular AI capabilities that teach the Elastic AI Agent how to detect, investigate, and hunt like a specialist. This is how they work, and why they matter for the SOC.

item.title: From plain English to production rule: AI-native Elasticsearch ES|QL detection in Elastic Security
item.description: Elastic Security now lets analysts describe a threat behavior in plain language and receive a complete, validated Elasticsearch ES|QL detection rule in return, no query expertise required.

item.title: Microsoft Defender wrongly flags DigiCert certs as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha
item.description: Microsoft Defender is detecting legitimate DigiCert root certificates as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha, resulting in widespread false-positive alerts, and in some cases, removing certificates from Windows. [...]

item.description: Wireshark release 4.6.5 fixes 43 vulnerabilities (38 CVEs) and 35 bugs.

item.title: Telegram Mini Apps abused for crypto scams, Android malware delivery
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a large-scale fraud operation that uses Telegram's Mini App feature to run crypto scams, impersonate well-known brands, and distribute Android malware. [...]

item.description: Google revamps bug bounties: Android rewards rise to $1.5M, Chrome payouts drop, shifting focus to high-impact, AI-resistant vulnerabilities. Google has announced a major overhaul of its Vulnerability Reward Programs...

item.title: Brace for the patch tsunami: AI is unearthing decades of buried code debt

item.description: Deep#Door hides a Python RAT inside a batch file, kills Windows defenses, survives via multiple persistence methods, and exfiltrates data through a public TCP tunnel. Security researchers at Securonix uncovered a soph...

item.title: Microsoft Shell Spoofing Zero-day Vulnerability
item.description: What is the Attack? A newly disclosed vulnerability, CVE-2026-32202, has emerged due to an incomplete patch by Microsoft for a previously exploited remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-21510). While the original updat...

item.title: Microsoft tests modern Windows Run, says it's faster than legacy dialog
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that Windows 11 is getting a new modern Run dialog with dark mode support and faster performance in a new preview build. [...]

item.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
item.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The activity has been codenamed...
